findings.title: learn:plan doc flow claims Task(tool="learn-planning-agent") but the implementation section uses ccw cli
findings.impact: Creates ambiguity for maintainers; readers may implement the wrong invocation path and miss retry/escaping logic.
findings.recommendation: Make the Execution Process match the actual invocation (ccw cli + retry helper), or implement the Task path and test it.

findings.title: ensurePhases fallback uses phase_name 'Phase 1' which violates learn-plan.schema.json enum
findings.impact: If assignPhases() import is missing or fails, the fallback produces a plan that cannot pass schema validation (blocking plan creation).
findings.recommendation: Map fallback phase names to schema enum values (e.g. Foundation/Core Concepts/.../Mastery).

findings.title: learn:execute passes JSON evidence inside single quotes without escaping
findings.impact: Evidence containing a single quote or large JSON can break the command or become an injection vector in bash.
findings.recommendation: Pass evidence via file or base64, or escape through a shared helper (like agent-caller escapeForSingleQuotes).

findings.title: learn:profile background parsing builds a bash command with unescaped JSON.stringify(text)
findings.impact: Background text frequently contains quotes/newlines; this can break argument parsing or be exploited if shell interpolation is possible.
findings.recommendation: Write background to a scratch file and use --file, or properly quote/escape the --text argument.

areas_remaining: Portability of file operations in learn:plan

leads.description: Check learn:execute phase upgrade persistence vs available ccw learn:* APIs
